description:
You are a junior data-analyst who has just received a small raw sales extract and must prepare two artefacts for your manager.

    The raw file is already present on disk at:

        /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv

    Content of /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv (7 lines of data + 1 header):

        Date,Region,Product,Quantity,Unit_Price
        2023-01-03,North,Widget,30,9.99
        2023-01-05,South,Gadget,20,14.95
        2023-02-10,East,Widget,45,9.99
        2023-02-14,West,Doohickey,50,4.50
        2023-03-01,North,Gadget,25,14.95
        2023-03-12,South,Widget,75,9.99
        2023-03-20,East,Doohickey,40,4.50

    Your manager wants to know:
        • The two best-selling products (by total quantity) in JSON format.
        • An alphabetical per-product summary in CSV format.
        • A minimal processing log.

    Required final artefacts (all inside a new directory that you must create if it doesn’t yet exist):

        1. /home/user/output/top_products.json
           • Exactly 2 objects in a JSON array.
           • Sorted in descending order of Total_Quantity.
           • Each object MUST have only the keys “Product” and “Total_Quantity” (capitalisation matters).
           • Exact whitespace is NOT important, but the field names and numeric values must match the example below:

                [
                  {
                    "Product": "Widget",
                    "Total_Quantity": 150
                  },
                  {
                    "Product": "Doohickey",
                    "Total_Quantity": 90
                  }
                ]

        2. /home/user/output/summary.csv
           • Must include a single header line followed by one line per distinct product.
           • Columns (in this order): Product,Total_Quantity,Total_Sales
           • File must be sorted alphabetically by Product.
           • Total_Quantity must be an integer.
           • Total_Sales must be written with exactly two decimal places.
           • Example of the precise expected structure:

                Product,Total_Quantity,Total_Sales
                Doohickey,90,405.00
                Gadget,45,672.75
                Widget,150,1498.50

        3. /home/user/output/process.log
           • Exactly two lines, no extra whitespace:
                Records_Read: 7
                SUCCESS

    What you need to do (conceptual steps — do NOT paste these commands; figure them out yourself):
        • Create the /home/user/output directory if it is missing.
        • Read /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv, count the rows, and compute total quantity and total sales (Quantity × Unit_Price) per product.
        • Produce the two output files in the formats described above.
        • Write the log file.

    Constraints & hints:
        • You are limited to common CLI utilities that ship with most Linux distributions (awk, grep, sort, cut, jq, printf, etc.). You do NOT have root access.
        • The entire task can be finished with roughly three to four shell commands if you choose your tools wisely.
        • Ensure numeric precision/formatting exactly matches the examples so that automated tests can perform a strict comparison.
        • Only create/modify files inside /home/user/output; all other directories must remain untouched.

    When everything is finished, double-check:
        – /home/user/output directory exists.
        – All three files exist and their contents match the specifications above.
truth:
Expected files and exact contents after successful completion:

    File: /home/user/output/top_products.json
    Contents (exactly, ignoring insignificant whitespace):
    [
      {
        "Product": "Widget",
        "Total_Quantity": 150
      },
      {
        "Product": "Doohickey",
        "Total_Quantity": 90
      }
    ]

    File: /home/user/output/summary.csv
    Exact text:
    Product,Total_Quantity,Total_Sales
    Doohickey,90,405.00
    Gadget,45,672.75
    Widget,150,1498.50

    File: /home/user/output/process.log
    Exact text (including newline after each line):
    Records_Read: 7
    SUCCESS

    Pre-existing file before agent starts:

    /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv   (contents listed in <task>).

    The automated test will:
        1. Verify presence of /home/user/output/top_products.json, /home/user/output/summary.csv, /home/user/output/process.log.
        2. Read each file and compare its exact contents to the strings given above (allowing for insignificant whitespace differences in JSON).
        3. Confirm that no additional files were created inside /home/user/output.
